name: Melbourne
summary: Melbourne is Australia's second-biggest city, sitting on a curving bay in the south. It is famous for rattling old trams, colourful laneway street art, and the giant Melbourne Cricket Ground where huge crowds cheer on football and cricket.
funFacts: Melbourne has the biggest tram network in the world, with around 250 kilometres of track and 475 trams clanging through the city streets every day.
Aussie Rules football was invented in Melbourne in 1858 so cricketers could stay fit in winter by chasing an oval ball around muddy fields.
The sticky brown spread Vegemite was invented in Melbourne in 1922, and many Aussie kids still eat it on toast for breakfast.
Locals say Melbourne has 'four seasons in one day', so you might need sunscreen, a jumper and a raincoat all before lunchtime.
Tiny little penguins waddle ashore each evening on Phillip Island near Melbourne in a famous parade watched by thousands of visitors.
thingsToDo: Stand under the big yellow clocks at Flinders Street Station, Melbourne's busiest train station for over 100 years.
Wander Federation Square and watch buskers and big-screen sport in the heart of the city.
Climb the Eureka Tower and zoom 88 floors up to peek down on the whole city below.
Cheer on a footy or cricket game at the giant Melbourne Cricket Ground, nicknamed 'the G'.
Hunt for rainbow paintings down Hosier Lane, Melbourne's most famous street-art alley.
Hop on a free City Circle Tram and rattle past parks, museums and old gold-rush buildings.

wildlife.name: Little Penguin
wildlife.description: World's smallest penguin, blue-grey and white, that waddles ashore at sunset on Phillip Island.

wildlife.name: Koala
wildlife.description: Sleepy grey marsupial that hugs gum trees and munches their leaves all day.

wildlife.name: Eastern Grey Kangaroo
wildlife.description: Big bouncy animal with a long tail and a pouch for its joey.

wildlife.name: Platypus
wildlife.description: Strange furry creature with a duck's bill and webbed feet that lays eggs in rivers.

localWord.meaning: Welcome, in Woiwurrung, the language of the Wurundjeri, Melbourne's First People.

images.alt: The grand yellow Flinders Street Station with its big clocks and dome glowing in Melbourne's city centre.
The angular sandstone and glass buildings of Federation Square in central Melbourne.
The grand domed Royal Exhibition Building with its gardens in front.
Outside view of the huge Melbourne Cricket Ground stadium.
The tall Eureka Tower with its gold-topped crown rising above the Yarra River.
A colourful Melbourne laneway covered in bright street-art paintings and light-box pictures.
A tiny blue-grey little penguin standing on a rock at Phillip Island.
A fluffy grey koala climbing a eucalyptus tree.
An eastern grey kangaroo grazing in the bush.
A wild platypus swimming with its duck-like bill at the surface of a river.
